channel.title: Ironline
channel.description: Stories about the people who build things.
channel.itunes:author: Ironline
channel.itunes:summary: Stories about the people who build things.

item.title: The Future Economy — The Frontier
item.description: Kirin leaves Sable River on a freight transport to Kesmark, the last Compact settlement before the frontier, then crosses on foot into the depopulated zone. Over two days she walks through empty suburbs maintained by autonomous systems, shelters for a night in an abandoned house, and arrives at a scavenger settlement in a repurposed commercial strip. There she meets Dez — a former Free Zone logistics coordinator who has lived in the empty zone for eight years — and the small, practical community she's built with Seun, Tov, and Caramel. After Kirin shows Dez the authentication key and the coordinates four hundred miles east, Dez agrees to guide her: nobody goes east, and that's what makes it interesting. They leave at midday. Tov gives Kirin a name: Cleo, sixty miles east, who knows the country further than anyone. Episode 3 of The Future Economy.

item.title: The Future Economy — What She Left Behind
item.description: One long day in Sable River. Kirin Saleh digs into her grandmother's past and finds a life carefully erased. The archivist Perun uncovers Adaeze's pre-Compact name and confesses his own place in her history. The letters are in a reconstructed language almost no one alive still reads. At a dinner with friends, a casual sentence reveals the Compact's paradigm blindness to Kirin for the first time. At an old-timers' club, a woman who knew her grandmother in a plateau settlement forty-five years ago tells her what to expect — and what not to. And on the pavement outside the elder-care facility where Adaeze worked for twenty years, a night-shift aide tells her about a song sung in an unknown language to a dying stranger, and the shape of her grandmother's whole quiet life suddenly makes a different kind of sense. Episode 2 of The Future Economy.

item.title: The Future Economy — The Box
item.description: Three weeks after her grandmother's death, Kirin Saleh finds a hidden box in the linen closet. Inside: a mysterious object that predates every system she knows, and a note that says "Be patient with what it asks of you." As a materials analyst in the Compact — a future economy built on real assets and verified physical work — Kirin can read any surface. But this one doesn't match anything in the database. An archivist traces it to a pre-Compact protocol pointing to coordinates four hundred miles into the empty zone. Then she finds the photograph: her grandmother, young and radiant, standing in front of a building that shouldn't exist. Episode 1 of The Future Economy, a serialized science fiction series set 100 years from now.

item.title: Ironline — Coming Soon
item.description: Stories about the people who build things. New episodes coming soon.
